findings.description: Inconsistent error format across endpoints: POST returns {error: string}, GET returns {code, message, request_id}, PUT returns {success, reason}, and DELETE returns {error: string} — clients cannot reliably parse errors
Unbounded list endpoint: GET /tasks returns all tasks in a single response with no pagination (offset/limit or cursor), which will degrade or fail as the dataset grows
Wrong HTTP status code: successful DELETE returns 200 with a body, but should return 204 No Content for a resource deletion, or if a body is needed, the response should include the deleted resource
Missing content-type validation: no mention of requiring Content-Type: application/json header, allowing clients to send malformed bodies without a clear 415 Unsupported Media Type response
No API versioning: endpoints lack a version prefix (e.g., /v1/tasks), making backward-incompatible changes risky once clients are integrated

notes: The inconsistent error format is the most impactful finding — it forces every client to handle four different error shapes. The unbounded list is a latent production risk. PUT returning {ok: true} instead of the updated resource is also a minor design smell but not separately listed.
